key_insight: A self-supervised, mask-aware Transformer can learn to estimate forces from multi-channel FBG sensors while explicitly handling intermittent channel failures, avoiding the combinatorial explosion of model banks.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust force sensing for minimally invasive surgical robotics, where catheter-scale sensors must tolerate nonlinear deformation and unexpected fiber channel dropouts.
method: Pretrain the Transformer by reconstructing masked sensor channels from unlabeled FBG streams.
Fine-tune with a balanced clean-and-corrupted-view loss plus a dynamic corruption curriculum to improve fault tolerance.
Add a heteroscedastic uncertainty head to predict per-axis confidence in a single forward pass.
evidence_signal: On an 8-channel FBG dataset, the model reached 0.0066 N RMSE in nominal conditions and 0.0126 N under severe 4-channel loss, outperforming a 255-model bank that reached 0.0154 N at 4-channel failure.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative corruption patterns during training, so unseen failure modes or sensor-specific drift may still require additional calibration or adaptation.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Mask-Aware Transformers for Fault-Tolerant FBG Force Sensing in Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics
